backlog.title: AI Governance: Invocation Envelope + Versioning
backlog.description: Create ai_invocations table and persist model/version/prompt-hash/params/input-summary/output-summary/status per AI call. Add constants AI_PROVIDER, MODEL_NAME, MODEL_VERSION, TEMPERATURE, PROMPT_HASH to shared aiClient.ts.
acceptance_criteria: Every callAiGateway() call produces an ai_invocations row
Row contains: request_id, timestamp, model, provider, version, prompt_hash (djb2 of system prompt), temperature, input_length, output_length, latency_ms, status, correlation_id, function_name
No PII stored in input_summary or output_summary (length only)
Admin can query invocations by function_name, date range, status
Replay mode: admin can view invocation metadata without PII

backlog.title: Open-Answer Evidence Ledger
backlog.description: Create assessment_open_answer_evidence table linking each open-answer evaluation to its AI invocation, rubric version, and scoring output.
acceptance_criteria: Each analyze-open-answer call creates an evidence row
Row links to ai_invocations.request_id
Rubric version stored (currently implicit v1.1)
Score breakdown persisted alongside final score
Evidence rows queryable by user_id, attempt_id, field_key
backlog.rollback: Drop evidence table, revert edge function

backlog.title: Email Reliability: Outbox + Idempotency
backlog.description: Create email_outbox table with idempotency keys. Refactor all 4 email-sending functions to enqueue then send with retry logic.
acceptance_criteria: email_outbox table stores: id, idempotency_key, template, recipient_hash, payload, status (queued/sent/failed/retrying), attempts, last_attempt_at, provider_response, created_at
No duplicate emails sent for same idempotency_key
Failed sends retry up to 3 times with exponential backoff
All 4 email functions (welcome, verification, challenge-invitation, referral-invite) use outbox
Delivery status queryable by admin

backlog.title: Observability: Audit Events Table
backlog.description: Create audit_events table for lifecycle event tracking. Emit events for key user actions: signup, assessment_started, assessment_completed, challenge_submitted, report_generated, deletion_requested.
acceptance_criteria: audit_events table: id, actor_id, actor_type, action, entity_type, entity_id, metadata, created_at
Events emitted for: user_signup, assessment_start, assessment_complete, challenge_submit, data_export, account_delete_request
No PII in metadata (user_id only, no names/emails)
Events queryable by action, actor_id, date range
RLS: only admin role can read audit_events
backlog.rollback: Drop audit_events table, remove emit calls

backlog.title: Freeze Guard Hardening
backlog.description: Populate ASSESSMENT_FREEZE_HASHES with actual computed hashes instead of null values. Add CI-compatible validation.
acceptance_criteria: ASSESSMENT_FREEZE_HASHES has non-null values for en, it, es
Any locale content change triggers FATAL console.error
validateAssessmentFreeze() called on app startup
Hash values documented in v1.2 release notes
backlog.rollback: Revert hash values to null (v1.1 behavior)

backlog.title: Payment Decision: Enterprise-Only Mode
backlog.description: Implement enterprise-only contracts mode: membership tier UI shows tiers but purchase buttons replaced with 'Contact Sales'. No Stripe integration in v1.2.
acceptance_criteria: MembershipSection shows tiers with pricing info
All 'Subscribe' or 'Upgrade' buttons replaced with 'Contact Sales' linking to email
No broken payment flows in any user journey
Feature flag ENABLE_SELF_SERVE_PAYMENTS = false
Business pricing page shows 'Request Demo' CTA

backlog.title: L3 Video: Feature Gate or MVP
backlog.description: Either fully implement L3 video recording/storage/playback or gate it behind a feature flag hiding it from candidates until ready.
acceptance_criteria: If gated: L3 invitations not sent, StandingVideoSession page shows 'Coming Soon' or redirects
If MVP: video uploads to challenge-videos bucket, business can play via signed URL, submission status updates correctly
No 'shell' feature exposed to candidates
Decision documented in plan

backlog.title: Psychometric Monitoring: Real-World Validation Logging
backlog.description: Log assessment completion events with anonymized pillar distributions for statistical monitoring. No PII.
acceptance_criteria: assessment_analytics table: id, field_key, pillar_scores (anonymized), completion_duration_seconds, item_count, created_at
No user_id stored — purely aggregate statistics
Queryable for: completion rate, mean/std per pillar, cross-pillar correlations
Dashboard or SQL export available

backlog.title: Release Governance: Version Metadata
backlog.description: Add PLATFORM_VERSION constant, v1.2 changelog, and change-control SOP document.
acceptance_criteria: PLATFORM_VERSION = '1.2.0' accessible in app
Changelog documented in plan HTML
Change-control SOP for future releases defined
v1.1 freeze still passes after all changes

backlog.title: AI Fallback Provider Strategy
backlog.description: Document single-provider risk. Add circuit-breaker pattern to aiClient for graceful degradation.
acceptance_criteria: If AI gateway returns 5xx 3+ times in 60s, circuit opens and returns deterministic fallback
Circuit auto-resets after 120s
Fallback behavior documented per function
Alert logged when circuit opens
backlog.rollback: Remove circuit breaker, revert to direct calls

backlog.title: Operational Metrics: Request Count + Error Rate
backlog.description: Add lightweight request/error counting to edge functions via structured logs parseable by Supabase log drain.
acceptance_criteria: Every edge function logs: {type: 'metric', function_name, status, latency_ms}
Error rate calculable from logs
P95 latency derivable from logs
No additional infrastructure required

backlog.title: GDPR Operational Playbook
backlog.description: Document DSR handling procedures, data retention policies, and logging boundaries.
acceptance_criteria: DSR playbook covers: access (data-export), deletion (delete-account), rectification, restriction
Data retention recommendations per table documented
Logging boundary rules: no email, no name, no answer text in logs
Decision-support disclaimer (Art. 22) included in plan
